title: Conjecture 1 (Zhang, Liu, and Wang)
statement_text: Every graph with maximum degree $\Delta \geq 3$ and no isolated edge has an AVD-colouring with $\Delta + 2$ colours.
context_text: Zhang, Liu, and Wang (2002) conjectured this optimal bound on the number of colours needed in an adjacent vertex distinguishing edge colouring. The condition $\Delta \geq 3$ is required because $C_5$ needs five colours and is the only connected graph known to require more than $\Delta + 2$ colours. Prior to this paper, Hatami proved the conjecture holds up to an additive constant by showing $\Delta + 300$ colours suffice for $\Delta > 10^{20}$.
paper_contribution: Proves that every graph with maximum degree $\Delta \geq \Delta_0$ (for some explicit constant $\Delta_0$) and no isolated edge has an AVD-colouring with at most $\Delta + 19$ colours, substantially improving Hatami's $\Delta + 300$ bound via the entropy compression method.
notes: Math extracted from PDF; (cid:62) rendered as \geq, (cid:54)= rendered as \neq — logical content is clear.
paper_title: Progress on the adjacent vertex distinguishing edge colouring conjecture

title: Open Problem: $\Delta + O(1)$ bound for neighbour sum distinguishing colourings
statement_text: It is an intriguing open problem whether a $\Delta + O(1)$ bound holds on the number of colours required for a neighbour sum distinguishing edge colouring.
context_text: A neighbour sum distinguishing colouring is a proper edge colouring $c$ with colours in $[\Delta+2]$ such that for every edge $uv$, the sum of colours incident to $u$ and that of $v$ are distinct — a strengthening of AVD-colourings conjectured by Flandrin et al. The best known asymptotic bound is $\Delta + O(\Delta^{1/2})$ due to Przybyło. The authors note that neither Hatami's proof for AVD-colourings nor their own entropy compression argument seems adaptable to this setting.
notes: Stated in running prose without a labelled environment; clearly posed as an open problem by the authors.
paper_title: Progress on the adjacent vertex distinguishing edge colouring conjecture

title: Informal Conjecture: probabilistic methods insufficient for the $\Delta+2$ bound
statement_text: One could suspect, as we do, that probabilistic methods such as the Local Lemma or entropy compression will probably not be enough on their own to prove the $\Delta+2$ bound from Conjecture 1, assuming the conjecture is true.
context_text: After reducing Hatami's $\Delta+300$ bound to $\Delta+19$ using entropy compression, the authors reflect on the limitations of probabilistic techniques for fully resolving the Zhang–Liu–Wang conjecture, noting that some extra colours seem inevitably wasted in any random process.
notes: Conjectural belief expressed in prose using 'one could suspect, as we do'; no labelled environment.
paper_title: Progress on the adjacent vertex distinguishing edge colouring conjecture
